evals.prompt: Run the codex review loop against main until it approves my changes
evals.expected_output: Claude sets up ralph-loop with the adversarial review prompt, --base main, --completion-promise CODEX_REVIEW_APPROVED, and --max-iterations. The loop invokes superpowers, runs codex adversarial-review --wait --base main, fixes findings, commits, and repeats until approval.

evals.prompt: I need to iterate on code quality for my feature branch, review against develop until codex signs off
evals.expected_output: Claude adapts the loop to use --base develop instead of --base main. Otherwise same workflow: ralph-loop drives iterations, codex adversarial-review runs each round, fixes are applied and committed until approval.

evals.prompt: Run an adversarial review loop focusing on error handling and concurrency, keep going until codex approves
evals.expected_output: Claude sets up the ralph-loop and passes focus text ('error handling and concurrency') to the codex adversarial-review command. The review is scoped to those concerns while still using the full loop workflow.
